setting.note: Central Mozambique in the years of the civil war. An old man and a boy shelter in a burnt-out bus on an abandoned road through the bush and read a dead man's notebooks, whose journey runs along the Indian Ocean coast around Beira and Sofala and back through the ruined interior. The country is real and the war is real; the exact road and camps are left unnamed.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Mia Couto, first published in Portuguese as Terra Sonâmbula, 1992. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Sleepwalking Land, Mia Couto (1992). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The burnt earth and rust of the abandoned bus on the road. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
